title: Counseling Muslims handbook of mental health issues
book: Counseling Muslims handbook of mental health issues

node_types.description: Stores demographic, religious, cultural, and psychosocial information about a Muslim client relevant to mental health care.

properties.name: age
properties.description: Client's age

properties.name: gender
properties.description: Client's gender

properties.name: ethnic_background
properties.description: Client's ethnic or cultural background

properties.name: country_of_origin
properties.description: Country or region of origin

properties.name: immigration_status
properties.description: Immigration or refugee status

properties.name: religiosity_level
properties.description: Degree of religious commitment or practice

properties.name: sectarian_affiliation
properties.description: Islamic sect or school of thought

properties.name: language_proficiency
properties.description: Languages spoken and proficiency

properties.name: acculturation_level
properties.description: Degree of adaptation to host culture

properties.name: family_structure
properties.description: Family composition and roles

properties.name: education_level
properties.description: Highest level of education attained

properties.name: socioeconomic_status
properties.description: Socioeconomic background

properties.name: presenting_problem
properties.description: Main mental health concern or reason for seeking help

properties.name: cultural_identity
properties.description: Self-identified cultural and social identities

properties.name: discrimination_experience
properties.description: Experiences of discrimination or Islamophobia

properties.name: modesty_practices
properties.description: Practices related to Islamic modesty (e.g., hijab, niqab, gender interaction norms)

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1: Islam, Muslims, and Mental Health
Chapter 4: Mental Health Interview and Cultural Formulation

node_types.description: Stores information about Islamic beliefs, practices, and values relevant to mental health and therapy.

properties.name: belief_or_practice
properties.description: Name of the belief or practice (e.g., tawakkul, salah, zakat, fasting)

properties.name: category
properties.description: Type (e.g., core belief, pillar, ritual, value)

properties.name: description
properties.description: Explanation of the belief or practice

properties.name: relevance_to_mental_health
properties.description: How this belief or practice impacts mental health or therapy

properties.name: associated_terms
properties.description: Related Islamic concepts or terms

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1: Islam, Muslims, and Mental Health
Chapter 2: Conceptualizations of Mental Health, Illness, and Healing
Chapter 3: Traditional Mental Health Coping and Help-Seeking
Chapter 6: Individual Psychotherapy/Counseling: Psychodynamic, Cognitive-Behavioral, and Humanistic-Experiential Models

node_types.description: Stores explanatory models of mental health, illness, and healing as understood in Islamic and cultural contexts.

properties.name: model_type
properties.description: Type of explanatory model (e.g., biological, psychological, spiritual, supernatural)

properties.name: description
properties.description: Explanation of the model

properties.name: cultural_variation
properties.description: How the model varies across cultures or communities

properties.name: implications_for_treatment
properties.description: How the model affects help-seeking and treatment

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 2: Conceptualizations of Mental Health, Illness, and Healing
Chapter 3: Traditional Mental Health Coping and Help-Seeking
Chapter 4: Mental Health Interview and Cultural Formulation

node_types.description: Stores religious, cultural, and traditional coping strategies used by Muslim clients for mental health concerns.

properties.name: strategy_name
properties.description: Name of the coping strategy (e.g., prayer, ruqyah, consulting imam)

properties.name: strategy_type
properties.description: Type (e.g., religious, family-based, community-based, traditional healer)

properties.name: description
properties.description: Explanation of the strategy

properties.name: context_of_use
properties.description: When or why the strategy is used

properties.name: effectiveness_evidence
properties.description: Evidence or case examples of effectiveness

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 2: Conceptualizations of Mental Health, Illness, and Healing
Chapter 3: Traditional Mental Health Coping and Help-Seeking

node_types.description: Stores information about community resources relevant to Muslim mental health support.

properties.name: resource_type
properties.description: Type of resource (e.g., mosque, imam, outreach program, school, support group)

properties.name: description
properties.description: Description of the resource

properties.name: accessibility
properties.description: How accessible the resource is to clients

properties.name: cultural_alignment
properties.description: Degree of alignment with Islamic and cultural values

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1: Islam, Muslims, and Mental Health
Chapter 3: Traditional Mental Health Coping and Help-Seeking

node_types.description: Stores information about psychotherapeutic models and their adaptation for Muslim clients.

properties.name: approach_name
properties.description: Name of the therapeutic approach (e.g., CBT, psychodynamic, Islamic counseling)

properties.name: description
properties.description: Description of the approach

properties.name: cultural_adaptations
properties.description: Adaptations made for Islamic or cultural alignment

properties.name: compatibility_with_islam
properties.description: Degree and nature of compatibility with Islamic beliefs

properties.name: evidence_base
properties.description: Evidence or case examples for effectiveness with Muslims

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 2: Conceptualizations of Mental Health, Illness, and Healing
Chapter 6: Individual Psychotherapy/Counseling: Psychodynamic, Cognitive-Behavioral, and Humanistic-Experiential Models
Chapter 7: Family Systems Therapy and Postmodern Approaches

node_types.description: Stores clinical considerations, accommodations, and barriers relevant to providing culturally competent care to Muslim clients.

properties.name: consideration_type
properties.description: Type of consideration (e.g., confidentiality, gender norms, dietary needs, language, stigma)

properties.name: description
properties.description: Explanation of the consideration

properties.name: clinical_implication
properties.description: How this affects clinical care or treatment planning

properties.name: recommended_accommodation
properties.description: Suggested accommodation or adaptation

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1: Islam, Muslims, and Mental Health
Chapter 4: Mental Health Interview and Cultural Formulation

node_types.description: Stores information about family structure, roles, and dynamics relevant to Muslim mental health.

properties.name: family_type
properties.description: Type of family (e.g., nuclear, extended, patriarchal, multigenerational)

properties.name: roles_and_hierarchy
properties.description: Roles and hierarchy within the family

properties.name: cultural_and_religious_influences
properties.description: How culture and religion shape family dynamics

properties.name: common_issues
properties.description: Common mental health or relational issues in the family system

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1: Islam, Muslims, and Mental Health
Chapter 7: Family Systems Therapy and Postmodern Approaches

node_types.description: Stores information about sociopolitical factors affecting Muslim mental health (e.g., Islamophobia, discrimination, migration, legislation).

properties.name: context_type
properties.description: Type of sociopolitical factor (e.g., discrimination, migration, legislation, media portrayal)

properties.name: description
properties.description: Explanation of the factor

properties.name: impact_on_mental_health
properties.description: How this factor affects mental health or help-seeking

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1: Islam, Muslims, and Mental Health

node_types.description: Stores information about Islamic legal sources and rulings relevant to mental health, family, and social issues.

properties.name: source_type
properties.description: Type of legal source (e.g., Qur’an, hadith, shariah, fiqh, ijma, qiyas, istihsan)

properties.name: relevant_ruling
properties.description: Specific ruling or guidance

properties.name: application_area
properties.description: Area of application (e.g., confidentiality, insanity defense, child custody, marriage, substance use, sexuality)

properties.name: interpretation_variation
properties.description: Variation in interpretation across sects or cultures

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1: Islam, Muslims, and Mental Health

edge_types.description: Indicates that a client uses a particular coping strategy for mental health concerns.

properties.name: frequency
properties.description: How often the strategy is used

properties.name: perceived_effectiveness
properties.description: Client's perception of effectiveness

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 2: Conceptualizations of Mental Health, Illness, and Healing
Chapter 3: Traditional Mental Health Coping and Help-Seeking

edge_types.description: Indicates that a client seeks help from a specific community resource.

properties.name: reason_for_help_seeking
properties.description: Reason for choosing this resource

properties.name: help_seeking_outcome
properties.description: Outcome or satisfaction with help received

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 3: Traditional Mental Health Coping and Help-Seeking

edge_types.description: Links a client to their family system, structure, and dynamics.

properties.name: role_in_family
properties.description: Client's role in the family system

properties.name: family_support_level
properties.description: Level of support provided by family

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1: Islam, Muslims, and Mental Health
Chapter 7: Family Systems Therapy and Postmodern Approaches

edge_types.description: Indicates that a client's mental health or identity is influenced by a sociopolitical context.

properties.name: impact_type
properties.description: Type of impact (e.g., stress, stigma, identity conflict)

properties.name: severity
properties.description: Severity of the impact

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1: Islam, Muslims, and Mental Health

edge_types.description: Indicates that a client holds or practices a specific Islamic belief or ritual.

properties.name: practice_frequency
properties.description: How often the belief or practice is observed

properties.name: personal_importance
properties.description: Importance of the belief or practice to the client

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1: Islam, Muslims, and Mental Health
Chapter 2: Conceptualizations of Mental Health, Illness, and Healing
Chapter 3: Traditional Mental Health Coping and Help-Seeking

edge_types.description: Indicates that a client uses a particular explanatory model to understand their mental health or illness.

properties.name: model_salience
properties.description: How salient this model is for the client

properties.name: model_combination
properties.description: Whether the client uses multiple models

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 2: Conceptualizations of Mental Health, Illness, and Healing
Chapter 3: Traditional Mental Health Coping and Help-Seeking
Chapter 4: Mental Health Interview and Cultural Formulation

edge_types.description: Indicates that a therapeutic approach requires specific clinical considerations or accommodations for Muslim clients.

properties.name: adaptation_detail
properties.description: Details of the adaptation or consideration

properties.name: risk_of_non_adaptation
properties.description: Risks if not adapted

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 6: Individual Psychotherapy/Counseling: Psychodynamic, Cognitive-Behavioral, and Humanistic-Experiential Models
Chapter 7: Family Systems Therapy and Postmodern Approaches

edge_types.description: Indicates that an Islamic legal framework informs or shapes a clinical consideration.

properties.name: legal_basis
properties.description: Islamic legal basis for the consideration

properties.name: interpretation_notes
properties.description: Notes on interpretation or application

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1: Islam, Muslims, and Mental Health

edge_types.description: Indicates that a community resource provides support or services to a client.

properties.name: support_type
properties.description: Type of support provided

properties.name: frequency
properties.description: How often support is provided

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 3: Traditional Mental Health Coping and Help-Seeking
